item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you remove pineapples beyond the boundary and then bring them back, do they lack the effects? Is the Foundation site built on a pier or something? Or is there a large enough area of the island outside of the boundary lines to conduct experiments?

item.title: Staff Post - Info - Open
item.content:encoded: If you aren't familiar with the tagging system, please refrain from tagging with the minimum. This makes it harder for the article to be spotted by the tagging team and makes it so that the article will be untagged for longer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Since it was no tag, I attached the minimum necessary tags.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The consistency of tone itself is really good, and the article itself describes a really cool anomaly so far, but there's not enough for me to vote. Maybe adding a more detailed exploration log in the place of the last paragraph? It doesn't have to be world altering or anything, but something to make the impact of the reveal at the end hit a little harder.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So do the pineapples SCP-4796-A turns into when it's captured or destroyed have the same properties as the anomalous pineapple it was created from? I'd recommend making a note of that.

Possibly even giving the pineapples with anomalous properties their own designation to differentiate them from non-anomalous pineapples, assuming the pineapples created from SCP-4796-A are in fact normal pineapples, rather than anomalous ones.

Other than that, solid weird skip. Take my +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think this leaned into random WTF very hard, but successfully. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I was a bit iffy on this, just because the pineapples felt like you were forcing the "randomness". Getting to the lizards actually sold it; it was very creative and a neat sort of vengeance incorporated into this, and the originality was clear to me.

Mega cool, +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm finding myself of a similar mind to T Rutherford, except that I found the random menagerie of effects took away from my potential enjoyment of those effects on their own. I feel like there's a lot here that you could have focused on, but instead you focused on none of those things and instead opted for volume of WTF.

This time, it fell flat for me, leading to a downvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SKYNAPPLES DAMN IT.

But really, this is… fun? The anomaly itself fairly nonsensical and the mystical hoodunkery seems fairly arbitrary. The glowing, the pineapple juice spinal fluid, the -A literally just collapsing into a pile of pineapples- it reminds me a lot of the Corn God, honestly, except there's just less narrative. The ending also leaves a good deal to be desired; It never really escalates, just meanders for a while.

Cute skip, but there's just not enough here. It's short, but there's no (tropical) punch. Novote.
This reminds me of the "suddenly pineapples" section of the ASDF movies. This confuses me. I like it. +1
